books.title: The School for Scandal
books.author: Richard Brinsley Sheridan
books.summary: Despite their best efforts, the rumormongers of 18th-century London are disappointed by their targets.

books.title: Catriona
books.author: Robert Louis Stevenson
books.summary: After coming into his fortune and evading his uncle’s schemes, young gentleman David Balfour continues his adventures in 18th century Scotland, helping a politically unwelcome friend escape into France, avoiding the hangman himself, finding his place as a gentleman in Edinburgh society, and falling in love with the daughter of a notorious Macgregor clansman.

books.title: Wonderful Adventures of Mrs. Seacole in Many Lands
books.author: Mary Seacole
books.summary: A British Creole woman provides medical assistance during the Crimean War after her travels around Panama.

books.title: The Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers
books.author: Diogenes Laërtius
books.summary: Brief biographies of the prominent philosophers of ancient Greece.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A collection of George MacDonald’s fairy tales, short stories, and novellas.

books.title: The Man of Destiny
books.author: George Bernard Shaw
books.summary: Napoleon engages in a battle of wits to reclaim some stolen letters.

books.title: Richard III
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.summary: King Edward IV’s brother, Richard of Gloucester, ascends to the English throne through deadly machinations.

books.title: Thuvia, Maid of Mars
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: When the beautiful Princess Thuvia of Ptarth is kidnapped, suspicion falls on John Carter’s son Carthoris, who sets out to rescue the woman he loves.

books.title: My Brilliant Career
books.author: Miles Franklin
books.summary: A young woman struggles against the intellectual confines of her rural Australian life in the late 19th Century.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: M. R. James
books.summary: A collection of ghost stories written by M. R. James.

books.title: South!
books.author: Ernest Shackleton
books.summary: A British expedition sets off to cross Antarctica, but when their ship is wrecked they have to face a long struggle for survival.

books.title: The Bellamy Trial
books.author: Frances Noyes Hart
books.summary: A man and a woman are on trial for the murder of his wife, her friend.

books.title: A Yankee in the Trenches
books.author: Robert Derby Holmes
books.summary: An American describes his experiences in the British Army in the First World War.

books.title: What Is Property?
books.author: Pierre-Joseph Proudhon
books.summary: Proudhon outlines the anarchist economic theory of mutualism.

books.title: Idylls of the King
books.author: Alfred, Lord Tennyson
books.summary: Twelve poems detailing the adventures of Arthur’s round table.

books.title: Black No More
books.author: George Schuyler
books.summary: A medical process that makes black people look white revolutionizes American society.

books.title: Journey to the Center of the Earth
books.author: Jules Verne
books.summary: Prompted by a mysterious Icelandic manuscript, a professor and his nephew, with the aid of a local guide, adventure to the center of the earth through the crater of a dormant volcano.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A collection of George MacDonald’s fairy tales, short stories, and novellas.

books.title: An Inquiry Into the Accordancy of War with the Principles of Christianity
books.author: Jonathan Dymond
books.summary: Dymond argues that war is incompatible with the teachings of Christianity.

books.title: The Path to Rome
books.author: Hilaire Belloc
books.summary: Hilaire Belloc takes a walking pilgrimage to Rome from his hometown in France, writing of the journey, sketching the sights, and digressing on a wide variety of subjects.
